name: Nebius Group N.V.

profile.description: Nebius Group N.V. is a full-stack AI cloud infrastructure company that builds large-scale GPU clusters and operates an AI-native cloud computing platform for training, deploying, and running AI models and applications. Rebranded from Yandex N.V. after selling its Russian assets for $5.4 billion in 2024, the company operates data centers across Europe and North America. Backed by a $2 billion NVIDIA equity investment and anchored by multi-billion dollar contracts with Meta and Microsoft, Nebius has rapidly scaled to $1.92B in core AI cloud ARR.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): The transformation from Yandex N.V. — Russia's dominant internet company valued at $30B at peak — into a pure-play Western AI cloud infrastructure provider within 18 months is one of the most dramatic corporate reinventions in tech history. Revenue surged from $118M (2024) to $530M (2025) to $399M in Q1 2026 alone, representing 684% YoY growth [1]. AI Deployment (2/2): Nebius IS the AI product — it operates GPU clusters for AI training and inference, with core AI cloud ARR reaching $1.92B (up 54% QoQ) and Nebius AI adjusted EBITDA margin of 45% in Q1 2026 [1]. Market Share (1/2): Nebius is gaining share rapidly as a leading 'neocloud' provider but is not yet the category leader — AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud still dominate total cloud spend. However, the $12B five-year Meta agreement and multi-billion dollar Microsoft deal validate its position as a preferred AI infrastructure provider [2][3]. Product Pipeline (1/2): Priority access to NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform, 1.2 GW secured for a new owned AI factory in Pennsylvania, and raised 2026 CapEx guidance to $20-25B (up from $16-20B) signal massive expansion [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Extraordinary investment intensity with $20-25B in 2026 CapEx and 1,543 highly technical employees, though R&D is primarily manifested through infrastructure buildout rather than traditional R&D spend [1].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): While Nebius lacks a formally published mission statement, Arkady Volozh's vision of building the infrastructure layer that enables the global AI industry is distinctive, specific, and validated by extraordinary personal sacrifice — renouncing Russian citizenship and divesting $5.4B in Yandex Russian assets to pursue this purpose [4]. The mission has survived the company's most extreme existential test. However, the company is only ~2 years old in its current form and the purpose hasn't been tested over decades. Community Impact (1/2): Nebius's 'sovereign-ready' AI cloud for EU healthcare and financial institutions demonstrates alignment with public interest and data sovereignty, plus the $1.7B UK investment creating local jobs and infrastructure [2]. No specific charitable programs documented. Employee Retention (1/2): The company has assembled leadership from top-tier firms — Marc Boroditsky (ex-Cloudflare) as CRO, experienced board members — but is too new for meaningful tenure data. The 1,543-person workforce includes former Yandex engineers who followed Volozh through the transformation [4]. Insider Buying (1/2): Volozh maintains a significant founder stake and NVIDIA holds a $2B equity position, representing major institutional conviction. However, insider selling has been notable — 22 sales and 0 purchases in the last 6 months, including CTO and CRO sales totaling millions [5].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics with 5-year CAGR excluded (company too new in current form). D/E of 132.4% is elevated but reflects the capital-intensive infrastructure buildout funded by $9.3B in cash and equivalents after raising >$6B YTD including $4.3B in converts [1]. ROE of 14.1% is near target, driven by Q1 2026 net income of $621M (though inflated by a $780.6M revaluation gain on equity securities). Revenue growth YoY of 684% scores at maximum [1]. Forward P/E of 603.5 scores at floor — the stock is priced for massive future growth at $218/share and $55B market cap against ~$878M TTM revenue. Profit margin of 93.1% is inflated by revaluation gains; adjusted EBITDA margin of 45% is the more meaningful metric. PEG ratio of 0.63 scores at maximum, reflecting the extraordinary growth rate relative to valuation [1]. The company's $45B backlog and $9.3B cash position provide strong financial runway.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Nebius IS AI infrastructure — it provides the GPU clusters, cloud platform, and tools that AI companies use to build, train, and run models. AI disruption is structurally impossible for a company whose entire business is enabling AI. The $45B backlog anchored by Meta ($12B five-year deal) and Microsoft (multi-billion dollar agreement) confirms that the world's largest AI developers depend on Nebius's infrastructure [2][3]. The 1.2 GW Pennsylvania AI factory and secured capacity of >1 GW position Nebius as essential physical infrastructure for the AI economy. The only risk within the AI ecosystem is that hyperscalers could build equivalent capacity in-house, reducing demand for neocloud providers — hence 8 rather than 9 on risk protection. AI Opportunity (1/1): 100% of Nebius's revenue comes from AI infrastructure services. Q1 2026 core AI cloud ARR reached $1.92B with 45% adjusted EBITDA margin, and management targets $3.0-3.4B in 2026 revenue [1].

infinite_game.summary: Arkady Volozh's decision to renounce Russian citizenship, sell Yandex's entire Russian business for $5.4B, and rebuild as a Western AI infrastructure company is one of the most audacious acts of existential flexibility in recent business history. The company demonstrates strong infinite game characteristics in its willingness to sacrifice and reinvent, but its youth (2 years in current form) and untested response to adversity limit the score. The Just Cause is authentic but unproven over time.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Nebius's purpose — building the infrastructure layer that enables the global AI industry — is genuine, distinctive, and validated by Volozh's extraordinary personal sacrifice (renouncing citizenship, divesting $5.4B in Yandex assets) [4]. The cause is clearly idealistic and inclusive — enabling others to build AI rather than building AI applications itself. However, the company is only ~2 years old in its current form, and the purpose hasn't survived multiple cycles or leadership transitions. A score of 3 requires decades of validation.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): Nebius has assembled a high-caliber leadership team from Silicon Valley and European tech firms, and approximately 1,000 former Yandex engineers followed Volozh through the transformation — a powerful signal of trust in the founder [4]. However, the company is too new for evidence of long-term organizational trust, and the small 1,543-person headcount against $20-25B in CapEx suggests heavy reliance on contractors and partners, limiting trust assessment.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Nebius explicitly positions itself as a specialized 'neocloud' alternative to AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud — acknowledging hyperscaler strengths while carving a differentiated proposition based on AI-native architecture and technical depth [2]. Management's competitive language focuses on being the best specialized AI infrastructure provider rather than defeating hyperscalers broadly.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): The Nebius transformation from Russia's dominant internet company to a Western AI infrastructure firm is a defining example of existential flexibility. Volozh and the board sold everything Russian — $5.4B in assets, decades of accumulated market position — to pursue the AI infrastructure Just Cause [4]. This was not incremental adaptation; it was a complete corporate reinvention driven by conviction, not desperation. The raised CapEx guidance from $16-20B to $20-25B further demonstrates willingness to bet big on the future.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): The $20-25B CapEx commitment while still in early profitability, the $4.3B convertible note dilution accepted to fund growth, the $12B Meta commitment, and the 1.2 GW Pennsylvania AI factory all represent costly long-term bets [1]. However, the company has not yet been tested by a prolonged AI spending downturn, GPU shortage, or customer loss — courage is easier when everything is growing 684% YoY.

investment_thesis: Nebius is one of the purest plays on AI infrastructure outside of NVIDIA itself — a company growing revenue 684% YoY to $399M in Q1 2026, with core AI cloud ARR of $1.92B and 45% adjusted EBITDA margin [1]. The company is backed by NVIDIA ($2B equity stake), contracted to supply Meta ($12B five-year deal) and Microsoft (multi-billion dollar agreement), and targeting $3.0-3.4B in 2026 revenue with a $45B backlog [2][3]. Founder Arkady Volozh's transformation from Yandex is validated by institutional conviction and customer commitments at unprecedented scale. At PEG of 0.63, the growth-adjusted valuation is reasonable for a company defining the AI neocloud category. The $9.3B cash position and raised CapEx guidance ($20-25B) demonstrate aggressive but funded expansion.
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on execution and valuation risk at scale. Nebius trades at $218/share ($55B market cap) on ~$878M TTM revenue, implying a Forward P/E of 603x — a valuation that requires flawless execution of the $20-25B CapEx buildout across multiple continents simultaneously [1]. The $4.3B convertible notes and continuous capital raises dilute existing shareholders. The 93% profit margin is inflated by a $780.6M revaluation gain; adjusted profitability is nascent. Insider selling has been notable — 22 sales and 0 purchases in the last 6 months [5]. Customer concentration risk is extreme: the Meta and Microsoft contracts represent the vast majority of committed revenue. Any slowdown in AI infrastructure spending by hyperscalers, GPU supply disruption, or data center construction delays could severely impair the growth trajectory. The company has never navigated a downturn in its current form.
key_catalysts: 2026 revenue hitting $3.0-3.4B guidance and adjusted EBITDA margins sustaining at 40%+ would validate the business model at scale and likely trigger institutional re-rating

NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform priority access in late 2026 provides next-generation hardware advantage and customer pipeline visibility for 2027, potentially extending the technology leadership gap over competing neoclouds

1.2 GW Pennsylvania AI factory and UK deployments coming online, expanding total capacity to support $45B backlog execution and enabling European sovereign cloud wins in healthcare and financial services
key_risks: Capital structure risk: $20-25B 2026 CapEx against $9.3B cash requires additional capital raises that will dilute current shareholders — the $4.3B convertible notes already represent significant future dilution at a company with $55B market cap

Customer concentration: Meta ($12B) and Microsoft contracts represent massive revenue concentration — loss, delay, or renegotiation of either would materially impair 2026-2027 revenue guidance and investor confidence

AI infrastructure spending cyclicality: if hyperscaler AI CapEx growth decelerates (as it has in prior tech cycles), Nebius's capital-intensive model with $20-25B committed could face utilization and margin compression with limited flexibility to reduce spending
